title: notholaena pteridiformis
intro: Notholaena pteridiformis, also known as the "Fern-like Club Moss," is a species of fern-like plant that belongs to the family Pteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Notholaena pteridiformis include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and supporting respiratory health, making it a popular remedy for colds and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
